milestones.title: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone
milestones.description: Promote the read-only users sub-app out of command-center into its own enterprise-tier mini-app at crm.apps.lastrev.com. Lands a paired idempotent migration that formally tracks the existing public.contacts table (RLS, updated_at trigger, indexes), scaffolds the app via create-app, ports the list/detail UI from command-center/users with rename UsersApp→CrmApp and header copy CRM, builds zod-validated server actions for full CRUD (create/update/delete contact, edit notes/tags), introduces a unified ContactForm component used by both the new-contact dialog and an edit toggle in the detail panel, ships an AI enrichment endpoint at /api/enrich gated behind crm:admin and rate-limited (30/hour/user) that runs Vercel AI SDK + @ai-sdk/anthropic generateObject (claude-sonnet-4-6) against a ContactInsights zod schema and persists to contacts.insights + contacts.last_researched_at, deprecates the existing external insights writer to prevent clobbering, removes the users tile from command-center MODULES, deletes the old sub-app directory and test, adds a temporary redirect stub at command-center/users/page.tsx pointing to crm.apps.lastrev.com (slated for removal ~30 days post-release), introduces a re-runnable scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts for live-schema drift detection, and ships unit + component + integration coverage for actions and the enrichment endpoint.

issues.title: Add scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts for live-schema drift detection
issues.body:
## Summary
Introduce a re-runnable script that introspects the live `public.contacts` table via PostgREST's OpenAPI endpoint and reports drift against an `EXPECTED_COLUMNS` map hard-coded in the script. Used as a pre-flight before authoring the contacts migration and wired into CI later.

## Background
The `contacts` table already exists in Supabase but is not tracked in this repo. Before writing an idempotent migration, we need to confirm the live shape matches what we plan to write. The script keeps the expected schema co-located with the migration and `lib/types.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script lives at `scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts` and is invoked via `pnpm tsx scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts`
- [ ] Reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` from env via `lib/env.ts` (or direct, since this is a script)
- [ ] Hits PostgREST OpenAPI endpoint to introspect column names, types, nullability for `public.contacts`
- [ ] Hard-coded `EXPECTED_COLUMNS` map mirrors `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/types.ts` Contact shape and the migration DDL
- [ ] Per-column output uses the four-state symbol legend: `✓` ok, `Δ` drift, `✖` missing in live, `?` extra in live
- [ ] Exits with non-zero status if any drift, missing, or extra columns are found
- [ ] Supports `--json` flag for machine-readable output
- [ ] README comment at top of file documents env requirements and exit codes
- [ ] Script is added to package.json scripts (e.g. `"audit:contacts": "tsx scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts"`) only if natural; otherwise documented in plan and run ad-hoc
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Run live-schema audit and reconcile drift before writing migration
issues.body:
## Summary
Execute `scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts` against the live Supabase database, capture any drift / missing / extra columns, and reconcile by either updating `EXPECTED_COLUMNS` + the proposed migration DDL or by planning a follow-up migration for legacy columns.

## Background
The migration must be idempotent and non-destructive against the existing live table. The audit guarantees the DDL we ship matches the live shape so applying the migration is a no-op for column shape and only adds RLS/trigger/indexes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pnpm tsx scripts/audit-contacts-schema.ts` runs cleanly with non-zero exit only when drift exists
- [ ] All `Δ` (drift) entries resolved by updating either the script's `EXPECTED_COLUMNS` or the planned migration DDL
- [ ] All `✖` (missing in live) entries resolved by either removing from DDL or accepting that the migration will add them on apply (idempotent `add column if not exists`)
- [ ] All `?` (extra in live) entries triaged: documented as legacy in plan comment, added to DDL, or scheduled for a follow-up drop migration
- [ ] Final audit run produces all `✓` output (or documented exceptions) before issue is closed
- [ ] Audit output captured in PR description for traceability
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Scaffold crm app via pnpm create-app and verify registry entry
issues.body:
## Summary
Run `pnpm create-app crm --name="CRM" --tier=enterprise --permission=admin --template=full` to register the app in `apps/web/lib/app-registry.ts` and scaffold the directory under `apps/web/app/apps/crm/`.

## Background
The registry entry is the single source of truth for proxy routing, layout access, and tier gating. The `full` template wires `requireAppLayoutAccess("crm")` automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Confirm `crm` slug is not already taken in `app-registry.ts` before running
- [ ] `pnpm create-app crm --name="CRM" --tier=enterprise --permission=admin --template=full` completes without error
- [ ] `app-registry.ts` contains: `{ slug: "crm", name: "CRM", subdomain: "crm", routeGroup: "apps/crm", auth: true, permission: "admin", template: "full", tier: "enterprise", features: {} }`
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/` directory exists with `layout.tsx`, `page.tsx`, `__tests__/page.test.tsx` from the `full` template
- [ ] `layout.tsx` invokes `requireAppLayoutAccess("crm")`
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (registry / lib-listing checks happy)
- [ ] Local proxy routing verified: requests to `crm.apps.lastrev.com` (or local equivalent) hit `/apps/crm/...`
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add paired contacts migration (RLS, trigger, indexes) — idempotent
issues.body:
## Summary
Land `supabase/migrations/<DATE>_crm_contacts.up.sql` + matching `down.sql` that captures the existing `contacts` table shape going forward. The up-migration is idempotent and non-destructive — `create table if not exists`, `do $$ ... $$` guards on policy creation, `create index if not exists`, `drop trigger if exists` before re-creating.

## Background
The `contacts` table predates this repo. We're formalizing it without risking data loss. Down-migration drops the trigger, function, policy, and indexes — but **not the table**, since the data predates the repo. RLS gates via `crm:admin` permission, not per-row ownership (org-shared contacts).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration pair created under `supabase/migrations/` with paired `up.sql` + `down.sql` (date prefix matches repo convention)
- [ ] `up.sql` uses `create table if not exists public.contacts (...)` with all columns from the plan DDL: id, name, email, phone, title, company, type (with check constraint), avatar, location, timezone, slack_id, slack_handle, github_handle, linkedin_url, twitter_handle, website, tags (jsonb default '[]'), notes, insights (jsonb), last_researched_at, confidence, source, created_at, updated_at
- [ ] `alter table public.contacts enable row level security;`
- [ ] Single permissive policy `"crm admins manage contacts"` for `all` to `authenticated` using `(true)` with check `(true)` — gating happens at the app layer via permission
- [ ] Policy creation guarded against duplicate-policy errors (do-block or `drop policy if exists` first)
- [ ] `set_contacts_updated_at()` trigger function created with `create or replace`
- [ ] `trg_contacts_updated_at` trigger created with `drop trigger if exists` first
- [ ] Indexes created with `create index if not exists`: `idx_contacts_name`, `idx_contacts_company`, `idx_contacts_type`, `idx_contacts_last_researched` (last with `desc nulls last`)
- [ ] `down.sql` drops trigger, function, policy, and four indexes — does NOT drop the table
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (`scripts/check-migration-pairs.ts` happy)
- [ ] Migration applied locally and re-applied successfully (idempotent verified)
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Port lib/types.ts and lib/queries.ts from users sub-app to crm
issues.body:
## Summary
Copy `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/lib/types.ts` to `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/types.ts` unchanged. Port `lib/queries.ts` and add a new `getContactById(id)` query alongside the existing `getContacts()`.

## Background
Types (`Contact`, `ContactInsights`, `CommunicationStyle`, `Personality`, `Interests`, sort/view enums) are unchanged. Queries continue to use `@repo/db/server` and parse `tags` + `insights` JSON fields; `getContactById` is needed by the enrichment endpoint and the edit/detail flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/types.ts` exists, identical to the source file (only the path changes)
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/queries.ts` exports `getContacts()` matching the existing behavior
- [ ] `getContactById(id: string): Promise<Contact | null>` added; uses `@repo/db/server` and parses `tags`/`insights` consistently
- [ ] Both queries wrapped in `withSpan` from `lib/otel.ts`
- [ ] No imports from `@/app/apps/command-center/...` — the crm app is self-contained
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add lib/schemas.ts with zod schemas for ContactInput and ContactInsights
issues.body:
## Summary
Centralize zod schemas for input validation. `ContactInputSchema` is used by server actions and the create/edit form; `InsightsSchema` is used by the AI enrichment endpoint via `generateObject` and mirrors the `ContactInsights` shape from `lib/types.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/schemas.ts` created
- [ ] `ContactInputSchema` covers all writable contact fields (name required; type enum constrained to the same values as the DB check constraint; tags array; optional socials)
- [ ] `ContactInputSchema` exports a TS type via `z.infer<>` named `ContactInput`
- [ ] `InsightsSchema` matches the spec from the plan: confidence enum (high/medium/low), summary (1..280), bestApproach (1..280), communicationStyle (4 nullable strings), personality (3 nullable strings + motivators[<=6] + stressors[<=6]), interests (professional[<=8], personal[<=8], sharedWithAdam[<=6]), conversationStarters[<=6], topicsToAvoid[<=6]
- [ ] Schema field shapes verified against `lib/types.ts` `ContactInsights` (no drift)
- [ ] Zod v3 syntax matches the rest of the repo
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add lib/actions.ts: createContact, updateContact, deleteContact server actions
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement server actions for full CRUD on the `contacts` table, matching the `cringe-rizzler` and `cc-flags` patterns. Each action validates with `ContactInputSchema`, writes via `@repo/db/server` (RLS-respecting), revalidates the path, and is wrapped in `withSpan`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/actions.ts` starts with `"use server";`
- [ ] `createContact(input: ContactInput): Promise<Contact>` validates input with `ContactInputSchema`, inserts, returns full row
- [ ] `updateContact(id: string, input: Partial<ContactInput>): Promise<Contact>` validates with `ContactInputSchema.partial()`, updates by id, returns updated row
- [ ] `deleteContact(id: string): Promise<void>` deletes by id
- [ ] All three actions use `@repo/db/server` (NOT service-role)
- [ ] All three actions call `revalidatePath("/")` (or the appropriate crm app path) after mutation
- [ ] All three actions wrapped in `withSpan("crm.<action>", { contactId? })` from `lib/otel.ts`
- [ ] Errors logged via `@repo/logger`, never `console.log`
- [ ] Type validation errors throw a structured error the form can render
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add lib/enrich.ts: AI enrichment via @ai-sdk/anthropic generateObject
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement the core AI enrichment routine that the `/api/enrich` route handler calls. Uses Vercel AI SDK + `@ai-sdk/anthropic` `generateObject` against the `InsightsSchema` from `lib/schemas.ts`. Model is `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Prompt is the v0 placeholder structure from the plan; treat as iterable.

## Background
The schema is the contract; prompt wording can iterate without changing the API. Grounded-only output rules in the system prompt prevent invented biographical claims.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/lib/enrich.ts` exports `enrichContact(contact: Contact): Promise<ContactInsights>`
- [ ] Uses `@ai-sdk/anthropic` provider with model `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- [ ] Calls `generateObject` with `schema: InsightsSchema` from `lib/schemas.ts`
- [ ] System prompt matches the plan's v0 wording (relationship-intelligence framing for Adam Harris / Last Rev, evidence-only rules, no private/sensitive content)
- [ ] User message templated from the contact fields per the plan (name, title, company, type, email, location, timezone, linkedin, github, twitter, slack, website, tags, notes)
- [ ] Function does NOT persist; persistence is the route handler's job
- [ ] Wrapped in `withSpan("crm.enrich", { contactId })`
- [ ] Errors from the AI SDK are surfaced to the caller with enough detail to log
- [ ] Implementation respects `@repo/auth`'s server-only constraints (no client imports leaked)
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add api/enrich/route.ts: POST endpoint with auth, rate limit, persistence
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement the `POST /api/enrich` route handler under `apps/web/app/apps/crm/api/enrich/route.ts`. Validates auth + permission, rate-limits per user, calls `enrichContact()`, persists `insights` + `last_researched_at`, returns the updated insights.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route at `apps/web/app/apps/crm/api/enrich/route.ts` exports `POST`
- [ ] Body: `{ contactId: string }` validated with zod
- [ ] Auth: `requireAccess("crm", "admin")` from `@repo/auth` (or the route-handler equivalent of `requireAppLayoutAccess("crm")`)
- [ ] Rate limit: `lib/rate-limit.ts`, 30/hour/user (keyed on auth subject)
- [ ] Loads contact via `getContactById(contactId)`; 404 if missing
- [ ] Calls `enrichContact(contact)` from `lib/enrich.ts`
- [ ] Persists via `@repo/db/server`: updates `contacts.insights = <result>` and `contacts.last_researched_at = now()`
- [ ] Response: `{ insights: ContactInsights, last_researched_at: string }`
- [ ] Wrapped in `withSpan("crm.enrich", { contactId })`
- [ ] Errors logged via `@repo/logger`; rate-limit hits return 429 with `Retry-After`
- [ ] Validation failures return 400 with structured error
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Port users-app.tsx to crm-app.tsx with rename and CRM header
issues.body:
## Summary
Copy `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/components/users-app.tsx` (410 lines) to `apps/web/app/apps/crm/components/crm-app.tsx`. Rename the `UsersApp` component to `CrmApp`. Change header copy from `"👥 Contacts"` to `"CRM"`. Keep the existing subtitle (`"{n} contacts · {m} with insights"`) untouched. Preserve search, filter (type/company), sort, grid/list view toggle, and detail-panel toggle.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/components/crm-app.tsx` exists with `"use client"` directive
- [ ] Component exported as `CrmApp` (not `UsersApp`)
- [ ] Header text: `CRM` (no leading emoji); subtitle preserved verbatim
- [ ] All existing search, filter, sort, view-mode functionality works as before
- [ ] Imports from `@/app/apps/crm/lib/...`, NOT from the old command-center path
- [ ] No inline hex colors introduced (token audit clean)
- [ ] Renders without console errors against an empty contacts list and a populated list
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add components/contact-form.tsx (shared create + edit form)
issues.body:
## Summary
New unified form component used by both the new-contact dialog and the edit toggle in the detail panel. Driven by `ContactInputSchema`. Built with `@repo/ui` form primitives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/components/contact-form.tsx` created with `"use client"` directive
- [ ] Props: `{ initialValues?: Partial<ContactInput>; onSubmit: (input: ContactInput) => Promise<void>; onCancel?: () => void; submitLabel?: string }`
- [ ] Fields cover all writable Contact columns (name required; type as a select matching the DB check constraint; tags as a multi-input; socials as optional text inputs)
- [ ] Client-side validation via `ContactInputSchema` (resolver pattern matches repo convention)
- [ ] Uses `@repo/ui` primitives (Form, Input, Select, Textarea, Button) — no Radix imports directly
- [ ] No hardcoded colors; uses theme tokens
- [ ] Loading + error states surfaced inline
- [ ] Submits via the `onSubmit` prop so callers wire up the appropriate server action
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Port contact-detail.tsx with Edit toggle, Delete, and Re-research actions
issues.body:
## Summary
Copy `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/components/contact-detail.tsx` (595 lines) to `apps/web/app/apps/crm/components/contact-detail.tsx`. Add: (1) an Edit toggle that swaps the read-only view for `<ContactForm>` bound to the selected contact and saves via `updateContact`, (2) a Delete button with confirm dialog wired to `deleteContact`, and (3) a Re-research button that POSTs to `/api/enrich` and updates the in-place insights view. If the file naturally splits into edit/view sub-components during the work, do that — otherwise leave the monolith.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/components/contact-detail.tsx` exists with `"use client"` directive
- [ ] Read-only view continues to render the existing detail layout (insights, communication style, personality, interests, etc.)
- [ ] Edit button toggles into `<ContactForm initialValues={contact} onSubmit={(i) => updateContact(contact.id, i)} />`
- [ ] Delete button opens a confirm dialog (`@repo/ui` Dialog) and calls `deleteContact(contact.id)` on confirm
- [ ] Re-research button POSTs `{ contactId: contact.id }` to `/api/enrich`, shows loading state, updates insights inline on success, surfaces 429 / error states
- [ ] Detail panel closes (or refreshes) after successful delete
- [ ] No hardcoded colors; theme tokens only
- [ ] No imports from old command-center path
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Wire CRUD UI into crm-app.tsx (New Contact dialog)
issues.body:
## Summary
Add a `+ New Contact` button to `crm-app.tsx` that opens `<ContactForm>` in a dialog and calls `createContact` on submit. After successful create, close the dialog and let the page revalidation refresh the list.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `+ New Contact` button rendered in the crm-app header area
- [ ] Click opens an `@repo/ui` Dialog containing `<ContactForm onSubmit={createContact} />`
- [ ] Successful create closes the dialog and the list reflects the new contact (via the action's `revalidatePath`)
- [ ] Validation errors render inline within the form (no toast-only)
- [ ] Cancel closes the dialog without state leakage
- [ ] Dialog accessible: focus trap, ESC closes, ARIA labels via `@repo/ui` defaults
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Update apps/crm/page.tsx to render CrmApp
issues.body:
## Summary
Replace the scaffolded `page.tsx` body with the server component pattern from the existing users sub-app: `getContacts()` then render `<CrmApp contacts={...} />`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/page.tsx` is a server component (no `"use client"`)
- [ ] Calls `getContacts()` from `@/app/apps/crm/lib/queries`
- [ ] Renders `<CrmApp contacts={contacts} />`
- [ ] Adds `loading.tsx` and `error.tsx` siblings (basic implementations using `@repo/ui` Skeleton / error layout)
- [ ] Page renders without errors against the live database in dev

issues.title: Port users smoke test → crm-app.test.tsx and add page.test.tsx coverage

## Summary
Move the smoke test from `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/__tests__/users.test.tsx` into the crm app, renamed and re-imported from the new location.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/__tests__/crm-app.test.tsx` imports `CrmApp` from `@/app/apps/crm/components/crm-app`
- [ ] Test asserts the component renders without crashing for an empty list and a populated list

issues.title: Add actions.test.ts: server-action contracts
issues.body:
## Summary
Unit-test `createContact`, `updateContact`, `deleteContact` against a mocked `@repo/db/server`. Assert input validation, span instrumentation, and revalidation calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/__tests__/actions.test.ts` exists
- [ ] Tests cover happy-path create / update / delete
- [ ] Tests cover validation failure (e.g. missing required `name`) returning a structured error
- [ ] Mock for `@repo/db/server` returns the inserted/updated row shape
- [ ] `revalidatePath` mocked and asserted called once per successful mutation
- [ ] No real network or DB calls in the test
- [ ] `pnpm test` green
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Add enrich.test.ts: enrichment endpoint with mocked AI client
issues.body:
## Summary
Integration-style test for the `POST /api/enrich` route handler with a mocked `@ai-sdk/anthropic` client. Assert auth gating, rate limiting, schema-shaped response, and persistence side effects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/crm/__tests__/enrich.test.ts` exists
- [ ] Mocks `@ai-sdk/anthropic` so `generateObject` returns a fixture matching `InsightsSchema`
- [ ] Test: unauthenticated request → 401/redirect (per `requireAccess` behavior)
- [ ] Test: missing `crm:admin` permission → 403
- [ ] Test: happy path → 200 with `{ insights, last_researched_at }` and DB write asserted
- [ ] Test: invalid body (missing `contactId`) → 400
- [ ] Test: missing contact → 404
- [ ] Test: rate-limit exceeded → 429 with `Retry-After`
- [ ] No real Anthropic API calls
- [ ] `pnpm test` green
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Identify and disable existing external insights writer
issues.body:
## Summary
Find the current pipeline that populates `contacts.insights` and `contacts.last_researched_at` from outside this repo. Disable it (or coordinate a hand-off date) so the new in-app endpoint isn't fighting it. Document the source and the disable steps in the PR.

## Background
From the plan: "the `last_researched_at`/`insights` fields are populated externally — that pipeline will be replaced by the new in-app endpoint." Leaving both writers running risks clobbering writes on contacts that get enriched manually.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] External writer identified (cron / external service / script — capture name + location in PR description)
- [ ] Disable mechanism executed (pause cron, disable function, comment out script — whatever fits the source) OR a hard cutover date scheduled and documented
- [ ] Confirmation that no automated process is writing to `contacts.insights` after the cutover
- [ ] PR description records the before / after state for audit
- [ ] Rollback plan documented (how to re-enable the external writer if the new endpoint underperforms)
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Remove users sub-app from command-center and add redirect stub
issues.body:
## Summary
Delete `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/` and `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/__tests__/users.test.tsx`. Remove the `slug: "users"` entry from the MODULES grid in `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/page.tsx:36`. Add a redirect stub at `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/page.tsx` that calls `redirect("https://crm.apps.lastrev.com")` from `next/navigation`.

## Background
Plan calls for the redirect stub to live at the old path so we can remove it cleanly later (~30 days post-release once analytics confirm zero residual traffic). `next.config.ts` redirects are global and harder to scope per-host, so we avoid that route.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/components/`, `lib/` directories deleted
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/__tests__/users.test.tsx` deleted
- [ ] `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/page.tsx` MODULES array no longer contains the `slug: "users"` entry
- [ ] New file at `apps/web/app/apps/command-center/users/page.tsx` whose body is `import { redirect } from "next/navigation"; export default function UsersRedirect() { redirect("https://crm.apps.lastrev.com"); }`
- [ ] Hitting `command-center.apps.lastrev.com/users` in dev redirects to `crm.apps.lastrev.com`
- [ ] No lingering imports from the deleted directory anywhere in the repo (grep clean)
- [ ] PR description includes a calendar reminder / follow-up issue link to remove the redirect ~30 days post-release
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone

issues.title: Final verification: pnpm lint, pnpm test, manual proxy + auth + enrich smoke
issues.body:
## Summary
Run the full local quality gate and a manual smoke pass before opening the bundled PR. Capture results in the PR description.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (registry, lib-listing, migration pairs, token audit, jsx-a11y)
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes (all new + existing suites green)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] Manual: `crm.apps.lastrev.com` (or local equivalent) loads, auth gate works, contacts list renders
- [ ] Manual: create / edit / delete contact succeeds; list refreshes
- [ ] Manual: Re-research button hits `/api/enrich`, persists insights, UI updates inline
- [ ] Manual: rate-limit hit on /api/enrich returns 429 (force by lowering threshold temporarily, then revert)
- [ ] Manual: `command-center.apps.lastrev.com/users` redirects to `crm.apps.lastrev.com`
- [ ] Manual: command-center MODULES grid no longer shows the Users tile
- [ ] PR description includes a checklist of these verification steps and any deviations
issues.milestone: 023 - CRM App — Promotion from command-center/users to standalone
